steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence introduces Spain's voting age (18), then pivots to Brazil's voting age (16). The blank must connect two facts that directly oppose each other.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘By contrast,’: signals opposition between two different facts. Spain's higher age vs. Brazil's lower age is a direct contrast. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘For example,’: would mean Brazil illustrates Spain's rule, but Brazil's age is different from Spain's, not an example of it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Firstly,’: implies a sequence or list, but the sentence is not listing steps. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Therefore,’: signals a logical consequence, but Brazil's voting age does not follow from Spain's. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
